title: Bart Gets An Elephant

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic opening sequence mishap
jokes.punchline: The familiar 'D'oh!' followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that defines the character - perfectly economical and integrated

jokes.text: Hmm, underpants... Bowling ball. More underpants.
jokes.setup: Homer cleaning out the couch cushions
jokes.punchline: The absurd items found, especially the repeated underpants discovery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's slovenly habits through found objects

jokes.text: We got a tip from a six-year-old that there's a dead Martian down there.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his plans for the day
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of taking intelligence from a six-year-old about aliens
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - shows his gullibility and adventure-seeking nature

jokes.text: And I'm going to the park to jam with the Little White Girls Blues Quartet.
jokes.setup: Lisa sharing her weekend plans
jokes.punchline: The incongruity of white girls playing blues music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of cultural appropriation and Lisa's pretentious musical interests

jokes.text: Son, when you participate in sporting events... it's not whether you win or lose, it's how drunk you get.
jokes.setup: Homer appearing to give fatherly wisdom about sportsmanship
jokes.punchline: Subverts the expected moral lesson with alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - takes familiar parental wisdom and corrupts it in character-perfect way

jokes.text: Push her down, Son.
jokes.setup: Family trying to leave while Marge blocks them
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual suggestion of violence against his wife
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical Homer line showing his selfishness

jokes.text: Oh, dear God, no! [Panting, Struggling]
jokes.setup: Homer learning he has to clean
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top physical reaction to housework
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's aversion to work

jokes.text: I'm tired. I'm hungry. Can't we just buy a new house?
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about cleaning
jokes.punchline: His solution is to buy a new house instead of cleaning
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's lazy logic and American excess mentality

jokes.text: Amen, Ernie.
jokes.setup: Tennessee Ernie Ford's '16 Tons' playing about coal mining debt
jokes.punchline: Homer relating personally to the working man's blues
jokes.explanation: Nice moment of Homer identifying with working class struggle through music

jokes.text: You never know when an old calendar might come in handy. Sure, it's not 1985 right now, but who knows what tomorrow will bring?
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to throw away old calendars
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical reasoning that 1985 might return
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic about time and hoarding behavior

jokes.text: 'Gomer upsets Sergeant Carter.' Oh, I'll never forget that episode.
jokes.setup: Homer reading old TV Guide
jokes.punchline: His sentimental attachment to the most generic TV episode description possible
jokes.explanation: Great satire of nostalgia for mediocre entertainment and Homer's simple pleasures

jokes.text: Pyle! Shazam!
jokes.setup: Homer remembering the Gomer Pyle episode
jokes.punchline: Wrong catchphrase - mixing up Gomer Pyle with Captain Marvel
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's confused pop culture knowledge mixing different references

jokes.text: Hey, Clinton, get back to work. Make me.
jokes.setup: Lisa playing saxophone outside
jokes.punchline: Bart treating her like Bill Clinton and her defiant response
jokes.explanation: Topical 90s political humor with Lisa's saxophone playing

jokes.text: Shove it, buddy.
jokes.setup: Homer reading warning label about ventilation
jokes.punchline: His dismissive response to safety warnings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer dismissal of reasonable safety precautions

jokes.text: I must destroy you!
jokes.setup: Homer apparently hallucinating from cleaning fumes
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration of war against cleaning supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's cleaning chemical-induced mania

jokes.text: Then stop screaming so loud. Okay. [Quieter Screaming]
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to stop screaming
jokes.punchline: Homer literally following instructions by screaming quietly
jokes.explanation: Excellent literal interpretation joke with perfect Homer logic

jokes.text: or a really stupid prize.
jokes.setup: Radio contest offering choice between money
jokes.punchline: The honest description of the alternative prize
jokes.explanation: Sets up the elephant prize with self-aware honesty

jokes.text: KBBL is gonna give me something stupid.
jokes.setup: Bart answering the phone with the contest phrase
jokes.punchline: He calls their prize stupid while trying to win it
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Bart's irreverent attitude toward the contest

jokes.text: Help! Help us! They stole our uniforms, guns and Tasers! Ooh, fry, piggy.
jokes.setup: Police emergency call
jokes.punchline: Criminals are using police tasers for fun while Wiggum calls for help
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about police incompetence with great Wiggum characterization

jokes.text: A full-grown African elephant.
jokes.setup: The 'stupid prize' being revealed
jokes.punchline: It's an actual elephant - completely impractical
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the stupid prize is genuinely absurd and drives the plot

jokes.text: Well, all that money sounds mighty tempting, Marty... but I think I'm gonna have to go with the elephant.
jokes.setup: Bart weighing his options in the contest
jokes.punchline: He actually chooses the elephant over $10,000
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - impulsive choice that creates chaos, driving the episode

jokes.text: We don't have a damn elephant.
jokes.setup: Bart actually choosing the elephant
jokes.punchline: Radio station's panic at their bluff being called
jokes.explanation: Good panic reaction showing the consequences of their fake prize

jokes.text: Stick it to the man.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts trying to talk Bart out of the elephant
jokes.punchline: Bart's rebellious attitude framed as anti-establishment
jokes.explanation: Good Bart line showing his natural rebelliousness against authority

jokes.text: Lisa's 'Stick it to the man' supporting Bart's elephant choice
jokes.setup: Lisa as the rational family member
jokes.punchline: Her rebellious support for an obviously bad decision
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's anti-establishment side overriding her usual practicality

jokes.text: Bart, with $10,000, we'd be millionaires! We could buy all kinds of useful things like... love.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Bart to take the money
jokes.punchline: His suggestion that love can be purchased
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer materialism and misunderstanding of emotions

jokes.text: Or double-ply windows. They look just like regular windows... but they'll save us four percent on our heating bill.
jokes.setup: Marge adding her practical suggestions
jokes.punchline: Her excitement over a tiny percentage savings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge practicality contrasting with Homer's absurdity

jokes.text: You all seem to be forgetting the most important thing... which is, that it's wrong to imprison an animal. Lisa, go to your room.
jokes.setup: Lisa making an ethical point about animal rights
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately dismissing her moral argument
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Homer's dismissal of ethics when they conflict with desire

jokes.text: We pay your principal $10,000 to pull down his pants... and keep 'em down for the rest of the school year.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts getting desperate to avoid giving an elephant
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous alternative offers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation with absurd sexual humor

jokes.text: What if we use the 10,000 to surgically transform Skinner here... into a-- some kind of a lobster-like creature?
jokes.setup: Continuing the desperate alternative offers
jokes.punchline: Even more bizarre transformation suggestion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation into body horror territory

jokes.text: Skinner's panicked 'That wasn't discussed with me' about the lobster transformation
jokes.setup: Outrageous surgical proposal
jokes.punchline: Skinner's bureaucratic concern about proper procedure rather than the insanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner response - more concerned about protocol than personal safety, brilliant character comedy

jokes.text: Where's my elephant? Where's my elephant?
jokes.setup: Bart's persistent demands
jokes.punchline: The repetitive chanting becomes a cultural phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Great running gag that becomes culturally significant within the episode

jokes.text: So, isn't that what we're all asking in our own lives? Where's my elephant? I know that's what I've been asking.
jokes.setup: DJ responding to the elephant song
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Where's my elephant?' as profound existential question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on how media creates meaning from nonsense

jokes.text: Well, hot dog, we have a 'wiener.'
jokes.setup: Automated DJ machine replacing human DJs
jokes.punchline: Robot making the same terrible puns
jokes.explanation: Good satire of automated broadcasting and formulaic DJ banter

jokes.text: DJ-3000's political commentary: 'Looks like those clowns in Congress did it again'
jokes.setup: Machine providing news commentary
jokes.punchline: Generic anti-Congress sentiment that applies to any situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of lazy political commentary that sounds informed but says nothing specific

jokes.text: It looks like it could gore. [Chuckles] It does look like Al Gore.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the elephant's appearance
jokes.punchline: Mishearing 'gore' as 'Al Gore' and agreeing
jokes.explanation: Decent political pun with Homer's characteristic misunderstanding

jokes.text: Marge, I agree with you in theory. In theory, Communism works. In theory.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concerns about keeping the elephant
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-intellectual dismissal using political analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line - pseudo-intellectual smugness about political theory he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Homer observing 'Look, he thinks he's people'
jokes.setup: Elephant displaying human-like behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's condescending observation about the elephant's dignity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer observation treating normal animal behavior as pretension

jokes.text: Oh, everything's cruel according to you. Keeping him chained up in the backyard is cruel. Pulling on his tail is cruel. Yelling in his ears is cruel. Everything is cruel. So excuse me if I'm cruel!
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Homer's treatment of the elephant
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive rant that accidentally catalogs his abuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where Homer accidentally proves Lisa's point while defending himself

jokes.text: Now I've had my head in an elephant, a hippo and a giant sloth.
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from the elephant's mouth
jokes.punchline: His casual cataloging of having been inside multiple large animals
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer line that raises questions about his past adventures

jokes.text: That bird! He's killing the elephant! Stop him! No, Dad, he's grooming him. Grooming him, eh? Oh, Homer, there's a bird on your head. I know. He's grooming me.
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about a bird on the elephant
jokes.punchline: His smug satisfaction at being 'groomed' by a bird
jokes.explanation: Good progression from panic to understanding to smugness

jokes.text: Homer's satisfied 'Mm, elephant fresh' comment
jokes.setup: Homer being groomed by a bird
jokes.punchline: His evaluation of the grooming experience like it's a product review
jokes.explanation: Homer treating wildlife interaction like a consumer experience

jokes.text: Thanks, bud. Appreciate it.
jokes.setup: Stampy apparently helping Bart in some way
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual thanks to an elephant
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Bart treating elephant like a regular friend

jokes.text: Technically, it's for a giraffe, but I think I can let it out a little.
jokes.setup: Reading a bill for an 'Oversized decorative poncho'
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical approach to mismatched animal clothing
jokes.explanation: Good Marge practicality in face of absurd situation

jokes.text: You'll have to raise my allowance to about $1,000 a week. Then that's what I'll do, smart guy.
jokes.setup: Bart demanding a huge allowance increase to pay for elephant costs
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing sarcastically, not realizing the financial implications
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Homer's poor financial planning

jokes.text: Your kid flew five feet. That counts as a ride. Two bucks.
jokes.setup: A child being thrown by the elephant
jokes.punchline: Homer treating potential injury as a successful business transaction
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Homer - prioritizing profit over child safety

jokes.text: Hey, Ma, look at that pointy-hairded little girl.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing visitors as slack-jawed yokels
jokes.punchline: Visitor immediately proving his point with poor grammar and insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect validation of Homer's insult through immediate example

jokes.text: This reminds me of that fat man I used to ride to work.
jokes.setup: Burns watching Homer ride the elephant
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to using humans as transportation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - casual cruelty delivered with aristocratic detachment

jokes.text: Look at this, Marge. $58 and all of it profit! I'm the smartest businessman in the world. Stampy's food bill today was $300.
jokes.setup: Homer bragging about his business acumen
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation that he's losing massive amounts of money
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline destroying Homer's pride immediately

jokes.text: Marge, please, don't humiliate me in front of the money.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out his financial losses
jokes.punchline: Homer treating money as a sentient observer to impress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Homer line - anthropomorphizing money to avoid embarrassment

jokes.text: Well, that was under our old price structure. Under our new price structure... your bill comes to a total of $700.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about paying $4 for multiple visits
jokes.punchline: Homer inventing retroactive price increases
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's desperation and poor business ethics

jokes.text: Well, it is and it isn't, if you understand what I mean.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Stampy is an elephant
jokes.punchline: Cryptic rejection implying the elephant isn't elephant enough
jokes.explanation: Absurd rejection with intentionally meaningless explanation

jokes.text: His boots are ivory. His hat is ivory. And I'm pretty sure that check is ivory.
jokes.setup: Lisa identifying the ivory dealer
jokes.punchline: Everything about him is made from elephant parts, even currency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd detail about ivory check

jokes.text: Lisa, a guy who has lots of ivory is less likely to hurt Stampy... than a guy whose ivory supplies are low.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about the ivory dealer
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about supply and demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely backwards but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: whale hunter, seal clubber, president of the Fox network
jokes.setup: Blackheart listing his previous jobs
jokes.punchline: Fox network president as equivalent to animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke insulting Fox (The Simpsons' network) as cruel as hunting

jokes.text: Then, if I know my geography, it's just 12 miles to Africa.
jokes.setup: Bart planning to run away with Stampy
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of geography
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - shows Bart's good intentions but poor planning

jokes.text: Oh, it's the four elephants of the Apocalypse. That's horsemen, Ned. Well, getting closer.
jokes.setup: Ned misremembering biblical reference about elephants
jokes.punchline: Homer's correction and Ned's weak justification
jokes.explanation: Good Flanders moment mixing religious knowledge with current situation

jokes.text: Look. It got Patty and Selma.
jokes.setup: Following destruction trail, Homer sees damage
jokes.punchline: His pleasure at his sisters-in-law being hurt
jokes.explanation: Dark but in-character Homer enjoying others' misfortune

jokes.text: Patty and Selma tornado aftermath: 'I feel it all the way up my skirt' / 'Ditto'
jokes.setup: Tornado damage to the twins
jokes.punchline: Their matter-of-fact discussion of intimate tornado effects
jokes.explanation: Somewhat crude humor that relies on sexual suggestion more than character

jokes.text: Wiggum. Yeah, right, buddy. Liquor store robbery, officer down. Sure. And I'm Edward G. Robinson. Waah!
jokes.setup: Wiggum dismissing elephant reports as fake
jokes.punchline: He also dismisses a real emergency while doing bad impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - ignoring real crime while focused on elephants

jokes.text: Many of you requested to be transferred to another peanut factory.
jokes.setup: Manager explaining elephant attack preparations
jokes.punchline: The existence of multiple peanut factories to transfer between
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about Springfield's peanut factory infrastructure

jokes.text: A deer! A female deer. Son, you're okay! And you led us to the precious ivory.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Bart with elephants
jokes.punchline: Sound of Music reference then immediately focusing on profit
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's priorities - brief parental concern then back to money

jokes.text: Yes, of course I would. Who wouldn't like that, to be part of the music scene?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Homer would like to be killed for piano keys
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for posthumous musical contribution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - finds death appealing if it involves fame

jokes.text: First, I'll just reach in and pull my legs out. Now I'll pull my arms out with my face.
jokes.setup: Homer sinking in tar, confident he can escape
jokes.punchline: His increasingly impossible escape plan
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's poor problem-solving skills

jokes.text: Ah, I'm alive! I'm alive! And I owe it all to this feisty feline. Dad, feline means cat. Elephant, honey. It's an elephant.
jokes.setup: Homer being saved by Stampy
jokes.punchline: His vocabulary confusion and family's corrections
jokes.explanation: Simple vocabulary joke showing Homer's educational gaps

jokes.text: On the other hand... who's to say what's right these days... what with all our modern ideas... and products?
jokes.setup: Homer seeming to do the right thing by giving Stampy to the refuge
jokes.punchline: Immediately second-guessing with meaningless philosophizing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer wavering between right and wrong with pseudo-philosophy

jokes.text: This is just what happened at the caramel factory.
jokes.setup: Family stuck together in tar
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual reference to previous caramel factory incident
jokes.explanation: Implies history of Simpson family mishaps in industrial settings

jokes.text: Well, animals are a lot like people, Mrs. Simpson. Some of them act badly because they've had a hard life-- or have been mistreated. But, like people, some of them are just jerks.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Stampy is attacking other elephants
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment that some animals are just jerks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation about nature vs. nurture ending with simple truth

tmdb_overview: Bart wins an elephant, but Homer won't let him keep it.
insight: Elephant premise sustains 67 jokes at 2.85-per-minute clip for classic character-driven chaos.
